original: Case 1: a monk asks whether a dog has Buddha nature; Chao-chou answers Mu.
english: The question wants doctrine; the answer gives a single syllable to inhabit.
spanish: La pregunta busca doctrina; la respuesta entrega una sola sílaba para habitar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 1
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 7-18. Short working rendering; source case centers on Chao-chou's Mu. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 2: not falling under cause and effect; not evading cause and effect.
english: Freedom is not escape from consequence; it is intimacy with consequence.
spanish: La libertad no es escapar de la consecuencia; es intimidad con la consecuencia.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 2
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 19-27. Candidate derived from the old Pai-chang fox koan and Wu-men's falling/evading contrast. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 3: whenever asked, Chü-chih raises one finger.
english: One gesture can hold a life, but the gesture is not the life.
spanish: Un gesto puede sostener una vida, pero el gesto no es la vida.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 3
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 28-32. Candidate focuses on the one-finger presentation and Wu-men's warning that realization is not the finger. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 4: why has the Western Barbarian no beard?
english: The why is not a request for an answer; it is the bell itself ringing.
spanish: El porqué no pide una respuesta; es la campana misma sonando.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 4
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 33-37. Candidate draws from Huo-an's question and Aitken's emphasis on staying with the why. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 5: hanging from a tree by the teeth, asked the meaning of Bodhidharma's coming.
english: Where speech loses life and silence evades duty, the whole body must answer.
spanish: Donde hablar pierde la vida y callar evade el deber, responde el cuerpo entero.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 5
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 38-45. Candidate compresses the dilemma without reproducing the full case. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 6 — The World-Honored One Twirls a Flower
original: Case 6: the Buddha twirls a flower; Mahākāśyapa smiles.
english: The teaching appears before speech, and one face cannot help but open.
spanish: La enseñanza aparece antes del habla, y un rostro no puede evitar abrirse.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 6
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 46-53. Candidate centers on silent presentation and recognition. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 7: after eating rice gruel, wash your bowl.
english: The meal is finished; the Way is exactly the next ordinary act.
spanish: La comida terminó; el Camino es exactamente el siguiente acto ordinario.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 7
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 54-59. Candidate grounded in Chao-chou's rice gruel dialogue and Wu-men's clarity verse. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 8: when cart wheels are removed, what is clarified?
english: Take away the working parts and the name of the vehicle begins to tremble.
spanish: Quita las partes que funcionan y el nombre del vehículo empieza a temblar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 8
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 60-63 plus TOC. Candidate is a conservative working rendering; compare against a public-domain Mumonkan translation before final promotion if exact case wording is needed. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 9: a Buddha sits for ten kalpas and still does not attain Buddhahood.
english: Practice does not manufacture Buddha; it exposes the error of attaining one.
spanish: La práctica no fabrica al Buda; expone el error de alcanzarlo.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 9
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 64-69. Candidate based on the nonattained Buddha exchange and Wu-men's realization/understanding contrast. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 10: Ch'ing-shui asks for alms while already full of the finest wine.
english: The one who comes empty-handed has already tasted what cannot be shown.
spanish: Quien llega con las manos vacías ya probó aquello que no puede mostrarse.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 10
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 70-75. Candidate uses short working rendering; avoids copying full case or commentary.

original: Case 11: Chao-chou tests two hermits who each raise a fist.
english: The same gesture is shallow in one place and bottomless in another.
spanish: El mismo gesto es superficial en un lugar y sin fondo en otro.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 11
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 76-80. Short working rendering; candidate derived from the paired hermit encounters and Chao-chou's different judgments. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 12: Jui-yen calls Master to himself and answers himself.
english: The one who calls and the one who answers must both stay awake.
spanish: Quien llama y quien responde deben permanecer despiertos.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 12
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 81-87. Short working rendering; candidate centers on self-calling, self-answering, and not being deceived. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 13: Te-shan comes to the hall with bowls in hand before the meal signal.
english: Before the signal, after the signal: the bowl reveals the timing of mind.
spanish: Antes de la señal, después de la señal: el cuenco revela el tiempo de la mente.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 13
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 88-93. Short working rendering; candidate based on Te-shan, Hsüeh-feng, and Yen-t'ou around the meal-hall timing. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 14: Nan-ch’üan demands one true word to save the cat.
english: When no one can speak from the whole body, even silence has claws.
spanish: Cuando nadie habla desde el cuerpo entero, incluso el silencio tiene garras.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 14
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 94-99. Short working rendering; candidate grounded in the cat dispute and Chao-chou's later sandal-on-head response. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 15: Tung-shan is told he deserves sixty blows, then asks where he erred.
english: A blow can be punishment, instruction, or the gate that was already open.
spanish: Un golpe puede ser castigo, enseñanza o la puerta que ya estaba abierta.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 15
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 100-106. Short working rendering; candidate based on Tung-shan's interrogation and the sixty-blows challenge. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 16 — Yün-men: The Sound of the Bell
original: Case 16: the world is vast; at the bell, monks put on robes.
english: The whole world is wide open, and still the bell asks for your robe.
spanish: El mundo entero está abierto, y aun así la campana pide tu túnica.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 16
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 107-112. Short working rendering; candidate drawn from Yün-men's bell-and-robe presentation. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 17: the National Teacher calls his attendant three times; three times he answers.
english: Being summoned is not yet hearing; answering is not yet arrival.
spanish: Ser llamado no es todavía oír; responder no es todavía llegar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 17
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 113-119. Short working rendering; candidate based on Kuo-shih's repeated calls and the reversal of disappointment. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 18 — Tung-shan's Three Pounds of Flax
original: Case 18: asked what Buddha is, Tung-shan answers three pounds of flax.
english: The answer refuses elevation by staying exactly with the weight at hand.
spanish: La respuesta rechaza elevarse quedándose exactamente con el peso presente.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 18
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 120-125. Short working rendering; candidate based on the three-pounds-of-flax response. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 19 — Nan-ch’üan: Ordinary Mind Is the Tao
original: Case 19: Chao-chou asks about Tao; Nan-ch’üan says ordinary mind is Tao.
english: Aim at ordinary mind and you miss it; cease aiming and it is not lost.
spanish: Apunta a la mente ordinaria y la pierdes; deja de apuntar y no se pierde.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 19
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 126-131. Short working rendering; candidate grounded in Nan-ch'üan's ordinary-mind dialogue. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 20 — Sung-yüan's Person of Great Strength
original: Case 20: the person of great strength does not lift a leg.
english: Great strength appears where nothing needs to be raised.
spanish: La gran fuerza aparece donde nada necesita levantarse.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 20
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 132-136. Short working rendering; candidate based on Sung-yüan's question about strength without lifting a leg. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 21: asked what Buddha is, Yün-men answers dried shitstick.
english: The sacred collapses into refuse, and the refuse refuses to be merely refuse.
spanish: Lo sagrado cae en el desecho, y el desecho se niega a ser sólo desecho.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 21
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 137-141. Short working rendering; candidate grounded in Yün-men's deliberately abrasive Buddha response. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 22: asked what else was transmitted with the robe, Mahākāśyapa calls Ānanda.
english: The call is the transmission; lowering the flagpole only confirms it.
spanish: El llamado es la transmisión; bajar el mástil sólo la confirma.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 22
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 142-146. Short working rendering; candidate grounded in Mahākāśyapa calling Ānanda and the flagpole instruction. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 23 — Hui-neng: Neither Good Nor Evil
original: Case 23: not thinking good, not thinking evil, what is the original face?
english: Before judgment forms, the face has already arrived.
spanish: Antes de que se forme el juicio, el rostro ya llegó.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 23
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 147-154. Short working rendering; candidate based on Hui-neng's original-face encounter; phrased to avoid duplicating the legacy seed row. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 24: speech and silence both become entangled in equality and difference.
english: Speak and you divide; stay silent and you freeze the division.
spanish: Habla y divides; calla y congelas la división.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 24
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 155-159. Short working rendering; candidate based on Feng-hsüeh's dilemma of speech, silence, equality, and differentiation. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 25 — Yang-shan's Sermon from the Third Seat
original: Case 25: in Maitreya's assembly, Yang-shan gives the sermon from the third seat.
english: Even in a dream assembly, the Dharma asks who is sitting here now.
spanish: Incluso en una asamblea soñada, el Dharma pregunta quién está sentado aquí ahora.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 25
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 160-165. Short working rendering; candidate based on Yang-shan's dream-sermon in Maitreya's hall. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 26 — Fa-yen: Two Monks Roll Up the Blinds
original: Case 26: two monks roll up blinds; Fa-yen says one gains and one loses.
english: The same motion opens the room differently in two bodies.
spanish: El mismo movimiento abre la habitación de modo distinto en dos cuerpos.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 26
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 166-170. Short working rendering; candidate grounded in Fa-yen's two-monks-and-blinds judgment. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 27 — Nan-ch’üan: Not Mind, Not Buddha, Not Beings
original: Case 27: Nan-ch’üan points beyond mind, Buddha, and beings.
english: When the three refuges become concepts, the gate moves elsewhere.
spanish: Cuando los tres refugios se vuelven conceptos, la puerta se mueve a otra parte.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 27
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 171-176. Short working rendering; candidate based on Nan-ch'üan's negating formula. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 28 — Lung-t’an: Renowned Far and Wide
original: Case 28: Lung-t’an lights a paper candle, then blows it out.
english: The lamp gives its teaching completely when it disappears.
spanish: La lámpara entrega su enseñanza por completo cuando desaparece.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 28
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 177-183. Short working rendering; candidate based on Lung-t'an, Te-shan, and the blown-out candle. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 29 — Hui-neng: Not the Wind; Not the Flag
original: Case 29: monks argue whether wind or flag moves; Hui-neng points to mind.
english: Neither wind nor flag is moving until the argument begins to move you.
spanish: Ni el viento ni la bandera se mueven hasta que el argumento empieza a moverte.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 29
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 184-188. Short working rendering; candidate based on the wind-flag-mind exchange. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 30 — Ma-tsu: This Very Mind Is Buddha
original: Case 30: Ma-tsu tells Ta-mei that this very mind is Buddha.
english: The medicine works until the patient makes a doctrine of the medicine.
spanish: La medicina funciona hasta que el paciente hace doctrina de la medicina.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 30
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 189-194. Short working rendering; candidate based on Ma-tsu's mind-is-Buddha teaching and its later reversal. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 31 — Chao-chou Investigates the Old Woman
original: Case 31: Chao-chou tests the old woman who directs pilgrims to T’ai-shan.
english: A road sign can be correct and still be the whole mountain testing you.
spanish: Una señal del camino puede ser correcta y aun así ser toda la montaña probándote.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 31
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 195-198. Short working rendering; candidate based on Chao-chou's investigation of the old woman's road instruction. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 32 — The Buddha Responds to an Outsider
original: Case 32: an outsider asks beyond words and beyond silence; the Buddha sits.
english: The answer neither speaks nor refuses speech; it simply occupies the question.
spanish: La respuesta no habla ni rehúsa hablar; simplemente ocupa la pregunta.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 32
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 199-203. Short working rendering; candidate based on the Buddha's wordless response to the outsider. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 33 — Ma-tsu: Not Mind, Not Buddha
original: Case 33: Ma-tsu says not mind, not Buddha.
english: After mind is used as medicine, not-mind becomes the next medicine.
spanish: Después de usar mente como medicina, no-mente se vuelve la siguiente medicina.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 33
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 204-207. Short working rendering; candidate based on Ma-tsu's corrective teaching after this mind is Buddha. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 34: Nan-ch’üan refuses to leave mind and Buddha as stable handles.
english: The more tightly mind names Buddha, the more Buddha slips the name.
spanish: Cuanto más la mente nombra al Buda, más el Buda se escapa del nombre.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 34
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 208-212. Short working rendering; candidate based on Nan-ch'üan's mind-and-Buddha case; compare against a public translation before live promotion. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 35 — Wa-tsu: Which Is the True Ch’ien?
original: Case 35: Ch’ien is divided between home and flight; which one is true?
english: When life splits into two stories, the question asks where the self was divided.
spanish: Cuando la vida se parte en dos relatos, la pregunta pide dónde se dividió el yo.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 35
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 213-220. Short working rendering; candidate based on the divided Ch'ien story summarized in Aitken's commentary. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 36 — Wu-tsu: Meeting Someone Attained in the Tao
original: Case 36: meeting a person of the Tao, neither words nor silence will do.
english: When neither speech nor silence is permitted, presence must find another mouth.
spanish: Cuando no se permite hablar ni callar, la presencia debe encontrar otra boca.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 36
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 221-225. Short working rendering; candidate based on Wu-tsu's encounter-with-an-attained-person challenge. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 37 — Chao-chou: The Oak Tree in the Courtyard
original: Case 37: asked why Bodhidharma came from the West, Chao-chou says oak tree.
english: The answer stands in the courtyard before the question arrives.
spanish: La respuesta está de pie en el patio antes de que llegue la pregunta.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 37
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 226-230. Short working rendering; candidate based on Chao-chou's oak-tree response. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 38 — Wu-tsu's Buffalo Passes Through the Window
original: Case 38: a buffalo passes through a lattice window; the tail does not pass.
english: Most of the self passes through; the smallest tail keeps the world unfinished.
spanish: Casi todo el yo atraviesa; la cola mínima deja el mundo inconcluso.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 38
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 231-234. Short working rendering; candidate based on Wu-tsu's buffalo-tail barrier. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 39: Yün-men catches a monk reciting borrowed illumination.
english: Borrowed light reveals itself by the shadow it cannot hide.
spanish: La luz prestada se revela por la sombra que no puede ocultar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 39
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 235-240. Short working rendering; candidate based on Yün-men's correction of borrowed words. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 40 — Kuei-shan Kicks Over the Water Bottle
original: Case 40: a water bottle cannot be named as water bottle; Kuei-shan kicks it over.
english: Where naming fails, one kick empties the argument.
spanish: Donde falla el nombrar, una patada vacía el argumento.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 40
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 241-247. Short working rendering; candidate based on the water-bottle test used to choose the mountain abbot. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 41: Hui-k’o asks Bodhidharma to pacify his mind; he cannot find it.
english: Bring the troubled mind forward, and its absence becomes the medicine.
spanish: Trae al frente la mente turbada, y su ausencia se vuelve la medicina.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 41
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 248-254. Short working rendering; candidate based on Bodhidharma's pacifying-the-mind exchange. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 42 — Mañjuśrī and the Young Woman in Samādhi
original: Case 42: Mañjuśrī cannot rouse the young woman from samādhi; another does.
english: Rank cannot wake what only exact intimacy can touch.
spanish: El rango no despierta lo que sólo la intimidad exacta puede tocar.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 42
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 255-260. Short working rendering; candidate based on the young woman in samadhi and Mañjuśrī's inability to rouse her. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 43: call this a staff and you oppose; do not call it a staff and you ignore.
english: The staff waits where affirmation and denial both become evasions.
spanish: El bastón espera donde afirmar y negar se vuelven evasiones.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 43
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 261-264. Short working rendering; candidate based on Shou-shan's staff dilemma. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 44: if you have a staff, Pa-chiao gives one; if not, he takes it away.
english: Having and lacking are both handled by the same staff.
spanish: Tener y carecer son tratados por el mismo bastón.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 44
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 265-268. Short working rendering; candidate based on Pa-chiao's give-and-take staff saying. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 45 — Wu-tsu: Who Is That Other?
original: Case 45: Śākyamuni and Maitreya are servants of another; who is that other?
english: Even Buddha and future Buddha point past themselves toward the unnamed one.
spanish: Incluso Buda y el Buda futuro apuntan más allá de sí mismos hacia lo innombrado.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 45
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 269-272. Short working rendering; candidate based on Wu-tsu's other question. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

artifact: Mumonkan 46 — Shih-shuang: Step from the Top of the Pole
original: Case 46: from the top of a hundred-foot pole, step forward.
english: Completion is still a perch until the next step leaves even attainment behind.
spanish: La culminación sigue siendo una percha hasta que el siguiente paso deja atrás incluso el logro.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 46
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 273-277. Short working rendering; candidate based on the hundred-foot-pole challenge. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 47: three barriers test nature, death, and the destination beyond death.
english: Seeing nature is not enough; the path asks where it stands when the eyes close.
spanish: Ver la naturaleza no basta; el camino pregunta dónde queda cuando se cierran los ojos.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 47
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 278-282. Short working rendering; candidate based on Tou-shuai's three barrier questions. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.

original: Case 48: the Buddhas of the ten directions enter one road to nirvana; where is it?
english: If all awakened ones enter by one road, the road must be under this very step.
spanish: Si todos los despiertos entran por un solo camino, el camino debe estar bajo este mismo paso.
recommendedEditionSource: Robert Aitken — The Gateless Barrier, Case 48
notes: Mode A extraction from Aitken PDF, pp. 283-286. Short working rendering; candidate based on Kan-feng's final road question. Copyright-gated: avoid long commentary excerpts.
